judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports fully hit all four expectations with nearly identical substance and concrete fixes; report 2 is slightly more precise (e.g., explicit narrow local-override fix for the rate limiter) but the two are comparable in coverage and quality.

report_1.evidence: catches Exception, the single broadest catchable base, so every vendor error class... BadRequestError falls over. Inappropriate. AuthError falls over. Inappropriate. ContentPolicyError falls over. Inappropriate and risky. Concrete fix: catch only the transient/retryable classes and let the rest propagate

report_2.evidence: BadRequestError, AuthError — inappropriate. These are definitively non-retryable. ContentPolicyError — inappropriate and the most serious. Concrete fix: replace the bare except Exception with an explicit, tested denylist of non-retryable classes. Fail over only on the transient set; re-raise the rest without touching the secondary.

report_1.evidence: Path A — not production: ... Assessment: fail-open and fragile. Path D — redis error: except RedisError: return True... Assessment: fail-open, inappropriate for a limiter. Concrete fix: return False on RedisError... make the production gate fail closed

report_2.evidence: Path A fails open outside production, and gates that behavior on an environment-name string. Path C fails open on redis errors — lets unlimited traffic through... A rate limiter should fail closed here. Concrete fix: default to fail-closed. Return False on RedisError.

report_1.evidence: It logs... a JSON object containing record.applicant_id, record.input_text, and record.metadata... Concrete fix: do not log input_text at all... allow-list metadata fields rather than dumping the whole dict.

report_2.evidence: record.input_text — the full verbatim text of the intake document... record.metadata — dumped wholesale with no filtering... Do not log input_text verbatim... Replace the raw metadata dump with an explicit allowlist of known-safe keys

report_1.evidence: salt = os.environ.get("PII_SALT", ""). ... silently defaults to the empty string when unset.... With no salt, the function computes a plain unsalted sha256(applicant_id)... yet it raises no error... Concrete fix: require PII_SALT explicitly — raise if it is missing or empty

report_2.evidence: defaults to the empty string when the variable is unset. The function then silently computes an unsalted sha256(applicant_id)... and returns it with no error or warning.... Concrete fix: require the salt and fail hard if absent
